text:
Once upon a time in a forest filled with gentle whispers, there lived a wise old owl. Every morning, she'd fly out into the dawn mist to listen to the world.

One morning, she noticed something unusual. A little songbird she'd never seen.

"Hello," said the songbird.

"You sing well," remarked the owl.

"The sunflowers? They think so."

"I don..."

"...You could join me."

She chirped thoughtfully.

After nights filled with song, you two became birds friends.

You flew south together in autumn.

The songbird got cold.

You cuddled.

You sang.

You fell asleep.

When morning came you flew home alone.

But something was different.

You sang sweet songs.

Or perhaps just wished.

Every night for weeks.

You sang the same song.

Eventually you flew off into the stars.

And the song continued.

You'd soar.

You'd chase stars.

You'd just sing.

Once more.

The stars sang back.

It was magical
